module-1-inventory-correction-cleanup.label: Module 1 Project: Inventory Correction and Cleanup
module-1-inventory-correction-cleanup.summary: Correct audited inventory values and remove only two approved test records through six cumulative steps.

query_sql.content:

SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (2, 4, 6);
SELECT id, price, status, is_test, notes FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (2, 4, 6);

sketch1.title: Inventory Correction and Cleanup Overview
sketch1.bodyMarkdown:
In this project, you will take on the role of an Inventory Data Steward tasked with correcting and cleaning up an inventory database. An audit has identified incorrect prices, unfinished statuses, and internal test rows that need to be addressed. Your goal is to create a SQL script that precisely targets these issues without affecting legitimate records.

The project will guide you through a series of steps where you'll preview the data, apply updates, and verify changes. This ensures that only the intended records are modified or removed, preserving the integrity of the inventory data.

### Example Scenario
Consider an inventory item with an incorrect price and status. You'll write a SQL query to update these fields based on specific conditions, ensuring that only the targeted item is affected. This approach prevents accidental changes to other records, maintaining data accuracy.

By the end of this project, you'll have a comprehensive script that not only corrects errors but also removes test data, leaving the inventory clean and accurate.

### Project Brief



**Scenario:** An inventory audit found incorrect prices, unfinished statuses, and internal test rows. Operations needs a cleanup script that changes only the records confirmed by the audit.

**Your role:** Inventory data steward

**Workspace:** Browser SQL editor with one entry file named query.sql, plus the schema browser, Tables tab, and deterministic results table

**Deliverable:** One cumulative query.sql correction-and-cleanup script that previews targets, applies scoped updates, removes confirmed test rows, and verifies preserved inventory.
